hits.title: The BRCA1 and BRCA2 Genes in Early-Onset Breast Cancer Patients.
hits.journal: Advances in experimental medicine and biology

hits.title: BRCA1: An Endocrine and Metabolic Regulator.
A high-throughput functional complementation assay for classification of BRCA1 missense variants.
Discovery of BRCA1/BRCA2 founder variants by haplotype analysis.

text:
Several papers discuss the function of BRCA1, ranging from its role in endocrine and metabolic regulation to its general biological functions and deficiencies.

### Literature Summary
Research regarding BRCA1 includes the following findings:
* **General Function and Deficiency:** Studies have specifically addressed the BRCA1 gene in the context of its function and deficiency [PMID:28884397].
* **Endocrine and Metabolic Roles:** BRCA1 has been identified as an endocrine and metabolic regulator [PMID:35432218].
* **Variant Classification:** High-throughput functional complementation assays have been utilized to classify BRCA1 missense variants [PMID:23867111].
* **Clinical Context:** BRCA1 and BRCA2 genes are studied in relation to early-onset breast cancer patients [PMID:29687286], and haplotype analysis has been used to discover BRCA1/BRCA2 founder variants [PMID:35671604].

### Evidence
